items.definition_en: A racket is used to hit the ball in tennis.
items.example_en: She bought a new tennis racket.

items.definition_en: A ball is a round object used in many sports.
items.example_en: The ball bounced over the net.

items.definition_en: A court is the place where people play tennis.
items.example_en: They met on the tennis court.

items.definition_en: A net is a thing in the middle of a tennis court.
items.example_en: The ball landed in the net.

items.definition_en: A match is a game played between two players or teams.
items.example_en: They watched an exciting tennis match.

items.definition_en: A game is a part of a tennis match.
items.example_en: He won the first game easily.

items.definition_en: A point is what you get when you win a part of the game.
A set is part of a tennis match made of games.
A serve is the action to start play by hitting the ball.
An umpire is a person who watches and controls the game.
items.example_en: The umpire called the ball out.

items.definition_en: Doubles is a tennis game with two players on each side.
Singles is a tennis game played by one person on each side.
items.example_en: The singles match lasted two hours.

items.definition_en: A tournament is a big sports competition with many matches.
items.example_en: He entered a tennis tournament last week.

items.definition_en: A winner is a person who wins a match.
items.example_en: The winner received a gold trophy.

items.definition_en: A coach is a person who trains players.
items.example_en: My coach teaches me how to play better.

items.definition_en: Practice means time spent improving your skills.
items.example_en: He has tennis practice every Friday.

items.definition_en: A spectator is someone who watches a tennis match.
items.example_en: Thousands of spectators cheered for the players.
